Description: Structure Android apps with MVVM presentation layer backed by Use Cases and Repositories
Do: Use ViewModel + StateFlow; separate data/domain/presentation layers; use Hilt for DI
Dont: Use Activity/Fragment for business logic; skip ViewModel; mix data and UI concerns

Description: Type-safe HTTP client with suspend functions and Moshi/kotlinx.serialization
Do: Use suspend functions; define typed API interfaces; use interceptors for auth/logging
Dont: Use raw OkHttp calls; use callbacks instead of coroutines; skip error handling

Description: Room provides compile-time verified SQL with LiveData/Flow support for reactive queries
Do: Use @Dao with Flow/suspend; define entities properly; plan migrations; use TypeConverters
Dont: Use raw SQLite; skip Room migrations; query on main thread

Description: Compile-time dependency injection with Hilt built on Dagger for Android
Do: Use @HiltViewModel @Inject @Module; scope properly; use @Provides for third-party deps
Dont: Manually create dependencies; use Service Locator pattern in large apps; use Koin without evaluation

Description: Store sensitive data with EncryptedSharedPreferences backed by Android Keystore
Do: Use EncryptedSharedPreferences for tokens; Android Keystore for keys; BiometricPrompt for auth
Dont: Store tokens in regular SharedPreferences; use plaintext files for credentials
Code Good:
val masterKey = MasterKey.Builder(context)
    .setKeyScheme(MasterKey.KeyScheme.AES256_GCM)
    .build()

val encryptedPrefs = EncryptedSharedPreferences.create(
    context,
    'secure_prefs',
    masterKey,
    EncryptedSharedPreferences.PrefKeyEncryptionScheme.AES256_SIV,
    EncryptedSharedPreferences.PrefValueEncryptionScheme.AES256_GCM
)

// Usage
encryptedPrefs.edit().putString('auth_token', token).apply()
val token = encryptedPrefs.getString('auth_token', null)
Code Bad: val prefs = getSharedPreferences('app_prefs', MODE_PRIVATE)\nprefs.edit().putString('auth_token', token).apply()\n// Plaintext, accessible with root

Guideline: Use JUnit5 with MockK for Testing
Description: Test Android code with JUnit5 and MockK for Kotlin-first mocking with coroutines support
Do: Use MockK for Kotlin mocking; use turbine for Flow testing; test ViewModel with TestDispatcher
Dont: Use Mockito in Kotlin projects; skip coroutine testing; test Activities directly
